Entries.UsedFor: Confirming HubSpot API base URL host
Entries.Excerpt: All APIs, whether legacy, new, or beta, use the same root path as before: https://api.hubapi.com/

Entries.UsedFor: Confirming HubSpot public apps use OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code flow
Entries.Excerpt: users are redirected based on the &redirect_uri= parameter in the original authorization URL, and a ?code= parameter will be appended to the URL. To generate your refresh and initial access tokens, you'll need the code from the ?code= parameter, redirect_url, client ID, and client secret.

Entries.UsedFor: Confirming HubSpot privately distributed apps Free/Starter rate limit (used as conservative default)
Entries.Excerpt: Privately Distributed Apps - Free/Starter: 100 / app per 10 seconds; 250,000 per account daily

Entries.UsedFor: Deriving HubSpot rate-limit window in ms (10-second burst window)
Entries.Excerpt: The documentation specifies the 10-second burst window figures: 100 / app per 10 seconds for Free/Starter privately distributed apps (10s = 10000ms).

Entries.UsedFor: Confirming HubSpot CRM list/search response envelope key
Entries.Excerpt: Response shape: { "total": 2, "results": [ { /* object data */ } ], "paging": { /* pagination info */ } } — 'results' is the wrapper key for returned objects.

Entries.UsedFor: Confirming HubSpot uses cursor-based pagination via paging.next.after
Entries.Excerpt: To access the next page of results, you must pass an `after` parameter provided in the `paging.next.after` property of the previous response.

Entries.UsedFor: Confirming HubSpot supports standard CRUD/search verbs across the public API (no global verb restrictions)
Entries.Excerpt: HubSpot's public CRM APIs expose standard list/search/create/update/delete operations across CRM objects; no global restriction documented at the integration level (per-object capabilities live on IO rows).

Entries.UsedFor: HubSpot uses OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code grant with long-lived refresh tokens that exchange for 30-min access_token via /oauth/v1/token
Entries.Excerpt: OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow. access_token expires in 30 minutes; refresh_token persists and is used to obtain new access tokens.

Entries.UsedFor: HubSpot accepts Bearer token in the Authorization header for both OAuth access tokens and private-app tokens
Entries.Excerpt: Include the access token in the Authorization header: Authorization: Bearer YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN

Entries.UsedFor: HubSpot CRM list endpoints support filtering by lastmodifieddate as a per-resource query parameter; not all endpoints support incremental
Entries.Excerpt: CRM objects expose lastmodifieddate property — use Search API with hs_lastmodifieddate filter to fetch records modified since a timestamp.

Entries.UsedFor: Per-resource incremental param name (used in search/list filters as hs_lastmodifieddate; modifiedSince is the canonical MJ-side label)
Entries.Excerpt: Filter records by lastmodifieddate to retrieve only those modified after a watermark.

Entries.UsedFor: HubSpot publishes webhook subscriptions for CRM events via /webhooks/v3/{appId}/subscriptions
Entries.Excerpt: Webhooks API lets your app subscribe to events that occur in a HubSpot account. Subscribe via POST /webhooks/v3/{appId}/subscriptions.

Entries.UsedFor: HubSpot signs webhook payloads with HMAC-SHA256; signature carried in X-HubSpot-Signature-v3 header
Entries.Excerpt: HubSpot includes an X-HubSpot-Signature-v3 header containing an HMAC-SHA256 hash you verify against your client secret.

Entries.UsedFor: HubSpot offers /batch/* endpoints (batch/create, batch/read, batch/update, batch/archive) on every CRM object
Entries.Excerpt: Use the batch endpoints (POST /crm/v3/objects/contacts/batch/create) to act on multiple records in a single API call.

Entries.UsedFor: HubSpot encodes API version in the URL path (e.g., /crm/v3/objects, /crm/v4/associations, /marketing/v3/forms)
Entries.Excerpt: HubSpot API versions appear in the URL path (e.g., /crm/v3/objects/contacts).

Entries.UsedFor: HubSpot custom objects are addressed by typeId (2-N) and custom properties live in the customProperties namespace on each object
Entries.Excerpt: Custom objects use typeId 2-N in path templates. Custom properties live under the customProperties namespace alongside standard properties.

Entries.UsedFor: Same marker pattern applies at field level; custom-defined properties appear under customProperties
Entries.Excerpt: Custom properties live under the customProperties namespace alongside standard properties.

Entries.UsedFor: HubSpot errors return {status, message, correlationId, category, errors:[...]} — single envelope with message at top level
Entries.Excerpt: Error responses include status (string), message (human-readable), correlationId, category, and an optional errors array.
